name: Brock 2013 Tadalafil BPH Without ED Quality-Of-Life Extract
population_or_context: Men at least 45 years old with BPH/LUTS, including a pooled subgroup without erectile dysfunction.
sample_size: 1089 total; 338 without ED and 751 with ED.
methods: Post hoc pooled analysis from three similarly designed randomized placebo-controlled 12-week tadalafil 5 mg once-daily BPH/LUTS studies.
population_profile: Men with BPH/LUTS, age at least 45, including a without-ED subgroup; indirect for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily for 12 weeks.
indication_context: BPH/LUTS treatment context; quality-of-life outcomes are urinary-symptom-specific.
study_design: Pooled post hoc subgroup analysis of randomized placebo-controlled BPH/LUTS trials.
safety_findings: Abstract reports tadalafil was safe and well tolerated in men without ED, with an adverse-event profile consistent with men with ED; detailed event extraction not completed here.
directness_to_target_population: Indirect because participants had BPH/LUTS and were older than the primary target population; quality-of-life endpoint is symptom-specific rather than global well-being.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Post hoc pooled subgroup analysis; IPSS-QoL and BII reflect urinary bother and BPH impact, not broad mood or life satisfaction.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil exposure; no class transfer needed, but BPH symptom-relief quality-of-life evidence should not be generalized to healthy well-being.
